summary.intent: Migrate the moltis web UI from vanilla JavaScript with HTM templates to TypeScript with JSX/Preact, improving type safety and code quality. Then iteratively improve the codebase with enums, typed RPC/WebSocket layers, component extraction, and file splitting.
summary.outcome: Complete TypeScript migration delivered on branch feat/typescript-migration: 142 TS/TSX files replacing 135 JS files, Vite build pipeline, 0 tsc errors, typed RPC (107 methods), typed WebSocket events (28-member enum), ChannelType enum, shared form component library (15+ components), all files under 1500 lines, and local-validate passing clean.

repo: The project uses `include_dir!` to embed the `crates/web/src/assets/dist/` folder into the Rust binary — `dist/` must be committed so `cargo build` works without Node.js
local-validate.sh auto-detects PR number via `gh pr view`; running it before pushing finds an old PR and can produce misleading results
File size limit (1500 lines) is enforced by check-file-size.sh but originally only covered .rs files — TypeScript files were not checked
Biome could not lint inside HTM tagged template literals, so a11y issues (missing button types, labels without controls) were invisible pre-migration and surfaced only after JSX conversion
The share page (share.html, share-app.mjs, share-theme-init.js) is a standalone mini-app separate from the main SPA and was intentionally kept as vanilla JS
GPG-signed commits require a YubiKey tap; automated commit flows get cancelled if the pinentry dialog doesn't get focus
git add -A accidentally pulled in working-tree changes from other sessions/branches unrelated to the migration, causing Rust compile failures that had to be reverted
The original websocket.js called `sendChat()` without importing it — a pre-existing bug that was masked by the non-strict module environment; the TSX version uses `S.chatSendBtn.click()` instead
Source maps added 27MB to dist/ (11MB without), bloating the embedded binary — Vite sourcemap must be set to false for production builds

code.finding: Vanilla DOM dialog builders (confirmDialog, shareVisibilityDialog, shareLinkDialog) duplicated the Preact Modal component — ~280 lines of imperative createElement that were refactored to use the existing Modal wrapper
chatParams typed as Record<string,unknown> but actual shape is ChatSendParams with text?, content?, _seq, model? — discriminated union ChatContentPart catches type/image_url field typos
Was 1593 lines; split into 5 sub-modules covering connection, reconnect, message routing, event dispatch, and frame parsing
Was 1732 lines; split into 3 sub-modules; Session/SessionMeta compatibility issues arose from subagent-introduced inconsistencies during migration
sourcemap must be false in build config to avoid 27MB of .map files being committed to dist/ and embedded in the Rust binary
Shared component barrel export for FormField, SectionLayout, ListItem, Badge, EmptyState, Loading, CopyButton, DangerZone, CodeDisplay
shiki_url template variable and DEFAULT_SHIKI_CDN_URL constant removed — shiki is now bundled by Vite at build time rather than loaded from CDN
Extended to cover .ts and .tsx files in addition to .rs files, enforcing the 1500-line limit on TypeScript

workflow: Subagents hallucinate imports — always verify generated code doesn't add imports that don't exist in the original (e.g., sendChat imported from ChatPage in websocket.ts)
Subagent output token limits prevent converting files over ~4000 lines in a single pass; files like SettingsPage (5656 lines) must be split across multiple agents
Running `biome check --write` followed by `biome check --write --unsafe` is the fastest path to auto-fix formatting + lint in two passes
TypeScript's `tsc --noEmit` and biome serve complementary roles: biome = formatting + lint rules (no type awareness), tsc = type errors (no formatting); both needed in CI
The `as unknown as X` pattern silences tsc without safety — tracking count as a quality metric (48 → 3) provides a concrete measure of type safety improvement
Parallel subagent batches (3-4 agents simultaneously) significantly accelerate large mechanical migrations but require a verification pass for import path consistency afterward

friction: YubiKey GPG signing blocks automated commits — pinentry dialog doesn't get focus in the terminal session, causing commit cancellations requiring manual intervention
git add -A pulled in unrelated working-tree Rust changes from other sessions, causing Rust compile failures (CredentialStorage, request_webauthn_host) that required a revert commit
API rate limits hit twice mid-migration ('You've hit your limit'), requiring the user to manually resume with 'proceed'
Subagents introduced incorrect imports (sendChat, htm/preact) and kebab-case component paths instead of PascalCase, requiring a post-migration fix pass
SettingsPage at 5656 lines exceeded subagent output token limits — required splitting into two agent calls and manual stitching
local-validate.sh auto-detects PR via gh pr view, finding stale PRs when the branch hasn't been pushed yet, making it unreliable for pre-push validation
biome `noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain` rule surfaced 4 `?.prop!` patterns that required manual narrowing fixes after unsafe auto-fix introduced type errors
Source maps were silently added to dist/ by Vite default config, bloating committed dist by 27MB before being caught
open_items: ~134 remaining `as HTML*Element` casts in querySelector/closest/createElement patterns — eliminating these requires migrating imperative registerPage+DOM pages to full Preact component trees
~32 `Record<string,unknown>` in genuinely dynamic contexts (channel configs, ws frames, gon data) — need per-endpoint type refinement as features are worked on
RpcMethodMap has many `unknown` placeholder response types — should be refined individually as each endpoint is worked on
SecuritySection, SshSection, ConfigSection, RemoteAccessSection, VoiceSection not yet converted to use shared form components — complex custom layouts need individual extraction
E2E Playwright tests may need import path updates after the migration
Delete the old vendored luxon.mjs and share-app.mjs once the share page is migrated (currently kept as-is)
SettingsPage sections not yet migrated to shared components: SecuritySection, SshSection, ConfigSection, RemoteAccessSection, VoiceSection
No enums added for route names, event names string constants beyond ChannelType — string comparisons remain in router.ts and event dispatch
Consider adding `vite build` as a step in local-validate.sh or CI to catch bundler errors before pushing
